purpose: Educational frameworks and learning science applied to IT curriculum design. Documents provide the pedagogical theory layer consumed by instructional design agents.

terminal_objective.prerequisite: Basic understanding of instructional design; familiarity with learning objectives
terminal_objective.concept: PBL is an instructional model where learners acquire competencies by producing authentic artifacts in response to real-world driving questions, assessed at Bloom Levels 3–6.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Design a multi-week IT project with a driving question, milestone checkpoints, critique cycles, and a public product deliverable aligned to industry workflows.
terminal_objective.market_value: High — PBL-trained learners produce portfolio artifacts that directly substitute for work experience in technical hiring.

educational_assets.title: Driving Question Authoring Workshop
educational_assets.deliverable: Write three driving questions for IT projects at beginner, intermediate, and advanced levels; evaluate each against Gold Standard PBL criteria

educational_assets.title: Design a 4-Week IT PBL Unit
educational_assets.deliverable: Complete unit plan: driving question, milestone rubric, critique protocol, public product spec, and individual accountability structure

educational_assets.title: PBL Failure Mode Analysis
educational_assets.deliverable: Analyze three provided 'broken' PBL unit designs; identify failure modes; propose targeted fixes aligned to evidence-based mitigations

educational_assets.title: PBL vs Problem-Based Learning Distinction
educational_assets.deliverable: 10-item classification quiz distinguishing PBL from problem-based learning, direct instruction, and traditional projects

terminal_objective.prerequisite: Familiarity with learning objectives and basic instructional sequencing
terminal_objective.concept: Scaffolding is temporary, targeted instructional support calibrated to the learner's ZPD, systematically faded as competency is demonstrated.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Implement tiered lab sequences (full worked example → skeleton code → blank problem) with explicit fading milestones and formative performance gates.
terminal_objective.market_value: Medium-High — scaffold design differentiates bootcamps producing job-ready vs tutorial-dependent learners.

educational_assets.title: Faded Worked Example Lab Series
educational_assets.deliverable: Three-part lab: (1) annotated solution to study, (2) skeleton to complete, (3) blank problem — all on the same core algorithm

educational_assets.title: Scaffold Audit Rubric
educational_assets.deliverable: Evaluate an existing IT lesson against six Wood-Bruner scaffold functions; score each; produce written improvement recommendations

educational_assets.title: Fading Schedule Design
educational_assets.deliverable: Map a 5-lesson unit progression from maximum scaffold to independence; justify each fading decision against learner performance criteria

terminal_objective.concept: CLT posits learning is constrained by working memory (~4±2 chunks); effective design minimizes extraneous load, controls intrinsic load, and maximizes germane load.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Audit any lesson for split-attention, redundancy, and over-complexity; apply chunking, worked examples, and spatial integration.
terminal_objective.market_value: High — CLT is the science behind why good courses outperform bad ones; essential for LLM-assisted tutoring system design.

educational_assets.title: Cognitive Load Audit
educational_assets.deliverable: Analyze a provided instructional slide deck; tag each slide as high/low extraneous load; rewrite two slides using split-attention and redundancy mitigations

educational_assets.title: CLT Effects Identification
educational_assets.deliverable: 12 instructional design scenarios — classify the applicable CLT effect (worked example, split-attention, redundancy, expertise reversal, modality)

educational_assets.title: CLT Redesign Challenge
educational_assets.deliverable: Redesign a 'cognitively overloaded' programming lesson using every applicable CLT principle; produce before/after comparison with justification

terminal_objective.prerequisite: None — applies at all curriculum design stages
terminal_objective.concept: Bloom's Revised Taxonomy classifies cognitive objectives across six levels paired with four knowledge types; enables measurable learning objective authoring and assessment alignment.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Write every learning objective as 'learner will [Bloom verb] [knowledge object]'; map each assessment to the stated Bloom level.
terminal_objective.market_value: High — Bloom alignment is required by institutional accreditation bodies and is the standard language for LMS metadata and course approval.

educational_assets.title: Learning Objective Rewrite Workshop
educational_assets.deliverable: Rewrite 10 vague objectives ('understand X', 'learn about Y') into measurable Bloom-aligned statements with correct action verbs

educational_assets.title: Bloom Level Classification
educational_assets.deliverable: Classify 20 assessment tasks by Bloom level and identify misalignments between stated objective and assessment type

educational_assets.title: Full Course Bloom Alignment Audit
educational_assets.deliverable: Audit an existing IT course; create a Bloom coverage heatmap; identify over/under-representation; produce a rebalancing proposal

terminal_objective.prerequisite: bloom-taxonomy; basic understanding of the IT job market
terminal_objective.concept: IT education requires concept-first sequencing, competency-based progression, authentic assessment, and deliberate bridging of the novice-to-practitioner gap using spiral curriculum and threshold concept identification.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Redesign any IT course by: identifying threshold concepts, removing tool-specific memorization from assessments, replacing final exams with portfolio or live-coding rubrics.
terminal_objective.market_value: High — IT-specific pedagogy produces measurably better hiring outcomes; directly relevant to ed-tech companies, bootcamps, and university CS/IT programs.

educational_assets.title: Threshold Concept Identification
educational_assets.deliverable: Identify 5 threshold concepts in a given IT curriculum; justify each selection; map the instructional consequences of misconception at each threshold

educational_assets.title: Authentic Assessment Redesign
educational_assets.deliverable: Convert a traditional IT final exam into a portfolio-based, authentic assessment suite with rubrics aligned to Bloom and industry readiness criteria

educational_assets.title: Novice-to-Practitioner Gap Analysis
educational_assets.deliverable: Map the five most common failure points in the novice-to-practitioner transition for a given IT skill area; propose curriculum interventions for each
